title: 💰 Backup Withholding: When Clients Hold Your Money
description:
When US clients are required to backup withhold from your invoices, how to avoid it, and how to recover withheld funds. Plus the UK CIS scheme for construction freelancers.

Personal finance, backup withholding, freelancer taxes, CIS UK, W-9 form 2026.

segments.voice: Most US freelancers have never heard of backup withholding until the day a client emails saying they have to withhold twenty-four percent from your invoice. It is a real IRS rule that catches freelancers off guard, especially first-timers. Today, when backup withholding kicks in, how to avoid it, and how to recover the money if it already happened.

What backup withholding is. Per IRC section 3406, when a payer makes payments to a contractor or freelancer and certain conditions are met, the payer must withhold twenty-four percent of the payment and send it to the IRS. The contractor reports the withheld amount on their tax return and gets credited against their tax liability.

When backup withholding is triggered. Three main triggers. First, the freelancer did not provide a W-9 form to the client, so the client lacks a valid TIN. Second, the IRS notified the client that the freelancer's TIN is incorrect, often via the B-Notice process. Third, the freelancer was previously notified by the IRS that they failed to report interest or dividends.

The W-9 fix. Most backup withholding situations resolve simply by submitting a properly completed W-9 form to the client. The form requires your name, business name if different, federal tax classification, address, and TIN. Sign and date it. Most invoicing systems and HR portals accept digital W-9 submissions instantly.

TIN matching, the underrated tool. The IRS provides a free TIN Matching service at irs.gov for businesses to verify that the W-9 information matches IRS records. If you submit a W-9 with the wrong name or TIN combination, even by typo, the client gets a B-Notice and must start backup withholding. Verifying ahead prevents the problem entirely.

EIN versus SSN trade-off. As a sole proprietor, you can use your SSN on a W-9. But exposing your SSN to every client increases identity theft risk. Apply for a free EIN online at irs.gov, takes ten minutes, and use that on every W-9 instead. The IRS treats them equivalently for tax purposes.

Recovering backup withholding. If a client withheld twenty-four percent and remitted to the IRS, the amount shows up on Form 1099-NEC box four. You report the gross income on Schedule C and the withholding on Form 1040 line twenty-five-c. The withholding gets credited against your total tax bill, usually creating a refund.

What if you never got a 1099. The client may have failed to issue one, but if they remitted backup withholding to the IRS in your name, the amount is in your IRS account. You can pull your IRS Wage and Income Transcript online at irs.gov to see what was reported. Anything in the transcript counts toward your refund claim.

B-Notice handling. If the IRS sends you a CP2100 or CP2100A notice about a TIN mismatch, your client receives a corresponding B-Notice and must start backup withholding within thirty business days unless you respond. Send a corrected W-9 immediately. The client can then stop the withholding for future payments.

Why this matters for cash flow. A freelancer earning fifty thousand dollars from one client subject to backup withholding loses twelve thousand from each payment cycle. That money does come back as a refund, but you wait months. The cash flow gap can sink a small business operating tight margins. Always handle the W-9 properly upfront.

Foreign freelancers and Form W-8BEN. If you are a non-US person providing services to US clients, you complete Form W-8BEN instead of W-9. Treaty country residents may qualify for reduced withholding under tax treaties. The default for non-treaty foreign service providers is thirty percent withholding, higher than backup withholding, but treaty rates can drop it to zero.

Real estate transactions. The Foreign Investment in Real Property Tax Act, FIRPTA, requires fifteen percent withholding when a non-US person sells US real estate. The buyer is the withholding agent. Foreign sellers can apply for a withholding certificate from the IRS to reduce the rate based on actual expected gain, which often materially lowers the withhold.

Gambling winnings withholding. Casinos withhold twenty-four percent on most jackpots over five thousand dollars per IRC section 3402(q). Lottery winnings over five thousand dollars are subject to the same. The IRS issues a W-2G to the winner, and the withheld amount applies as a credit on the tax return.

Common scenarios where freelancers hit backup withholding. New client onboarding without proper W-9 setup, switching from one entity type to another mid-year and forgetting to update the W-9, ignoring an IRS notice about a prior year reporting issue. Each of these is preventable with basic record keeping and prompt response to IRS mail.

How clients view this. From the client's perspective, getting a W-9 from every contractor and matching the TIN is administrative overhead. Many large clients use vendor onboarding systems like Coupa, Bill, or Stampli that automate W-9 collection and TIN matching. Smaller clients sometimes drop the ball, which is when issues start.

What to do if a client withholds incorrectly. If you provided a valid W-9 and the client still withheld twenty-four percent, you have to take that up with the client first. The withheld amount is already with the IRS, so you cannot recover it directly from the client unless they reverse the remittance, which is rare. Better to apply it as a credit at year-end.

UK readers, the Construction Industry Scheme. CIS applies to construction work in the UK. Contractors deduct money from a subcontractor's payments and send it to HMRC, similar to backup withholding. The standard deduction is twenty percent for registered subcontractors and thirty percent for unregistered ones. Subcontractors can also apply for gross payment status if they meet eligibility.

How CIS gross payment works. Subcontractors with annual turnover above thirty thousand pounds, a clean tax compliance record, and an established UK presence can apply for gross payment status. With gross payment, contractors pay you in full, no deduction. You report the income on your Self Assessment and pay tax in the normal way.

Withholding from royalties and rents. Per IRC section 1441, payments to non-US persons of royalties or rental income are subject to thirty percent withholding by default. Tax treaties often reduce this to ten or fifteen percent. Authors with foreign book sales, software companies licensing tech internationally, and US property owners renting to non-US persons all encounter this.

Documentation to keep. All W-9 forms you submitted, all client confirmations of receipt, all 1099-NEC and 1099-MISC forms received, all IRS notices, all bank deposit records showing payments received. The IRS audit window for 1099 issues can extend up to six years if there is significant under-reporting, so keep records that long.

TIN scrubbing tools. Software like Trolley, Track1099, and Avalara 1099 handle W-9 collection and TIN matching automatically. For freelancers managing multiple clients, these tools reduce administrative work. For clients managing many contractors, they prevent backup withholding compliance failures and the penalties that follow.

When the IRS audits a client for backup withholding. The IRS can hold the client liable for the entire amount that should have been withheld plus penalties if they failed to backup withhold when required. So clients have a strong incentive to follow the rules strictly. This is why some clients are so insistent on getting W-9 paperwork before processing your first invoice.

The bottom line. Backup withholding is preventable in nearly every case. Submit a properly filled-out W-9 with the correct TIN. Verify TIN matching with your client if they offer it. Respond promptly to any IRS notice about reporting issues. If withholding does happen, claim it as a credit on your tax return and recover the cash through your refund.

Subscribe to FinanzasClara and hit the bell. What's coming next week, the exact email template a freelancer used to push back on incorrect backup withholding from a client and got it reversed in two weeks. Drop a comment with the most confusing tax form you have ever received and I will break it down step by step.
